preamble: The transformation of the alveolar lateral approximant [l] into the voiceless uvular plosive [q] is a documented instance of phonetic assimilation, particularly notable in the historical development of Northwest Semitic languages. This shift typically occurs when [l] is adjacent to a uvular consonant, resulting in the lateral sound adopting the place and manner of articulation of its uvular neighbor.

examples.note: in the verb root *lqḥ 'take', as seen in *yilqaḥ- > yqḥ 'he will take'
in the verb root *lqḥ 'take', as seen in *yilqaḥ > yiqqaḥ 'he will take'

sources: Faber, A. (1997). The Semitic Languages. In R. Hetzron (Ed.), The Semitic Languages (pp. 3-15). Routledge.
